critic_raw:
BASELINE ASSESSMENT:

The paper's central comparison (34% vs. 22% non-returner capture) compares a model evaluated in a supported intervention context against a heuristic evaluated in a prior year without the support program. The new model identified students who then received tutoring and microgrants, which changed spring re-enrollment probability — making the outcome a post-treatment measure rather than a clean attrition risk label.

Three issues are present: (1) the outcome label was altered by the model-driven intervention; (2) the comparison spans years with different support availability, pandemic attendance rules, and cohort characteristics; (3) using spring re-enrollment after support program delivery as the accuracy criterion is methodologically post-treatment.

The model may be a useful tool for targeting retention support. But the paper's claim that it 'predicts attrition better' than the old heuristic is not established because no same-cohort, pre-support comparison exists.

Verdict: critique_wins.
